name: aligned_zero_size
reason: If aligned(addr, 0) is provable for all addr, then any code relying on aligned as a meaningful divisibility check is unsound when size is zero

name: inv_allows_empty_arch
reason: A zero-layer architecture is degenerate and meaningless; if inv() accepts it, downstream code assuming at least one layer may be unsound

name: upper_vaddr_collapses_to_base
code:
proof fn phi_3_upper_vaddr_collapses_to_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) == base,
{
}
reason: If upper_vaddr equals base, the address range for a layer is empty, breaking all bounds checks that rely on upper_vaddr as an exclusive upper bound

name: entry_base_not_strictly_monotone
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_base_not_strictly_monotone(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx + 1) <= arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx),
{
}
reason: If a higher index yields a lower-or-equal entry base, page table entries would overlap or go backwards, corrupting the address space layout

name: entry_size_increases_at_deeper_layer
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_size_increases_at_deeper_layer(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(i) < arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: If a parent layer's entry size is smaller than its child's, the hierarchical decomposition is inverted and page table walks would be inconsistent
